Scenario.title: Operation CuckooBees: Communications Phishing Attack
Scenario.overview: Communications sector organizations Simple This scenario depicts a targeted attack campaign named Operation CuckooBees, focusing on disrupting communication services through basic phishing techniques aimed at untrained personnel.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: To conduct reconnaissance, deploy phishing tools, and achieve impact through service disruption and data exfiltration

Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Command Execution (Mechanism), File Metadata (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2017-6744 (Suggested): The Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) subsystem of Cisco IOS 1 contains a vulnerability that allows an authenticated remote attacker to send crafted packets for unauthorized code execution or system reload
stage.capec: CAPEC-164: Mobile Phishing — An adversary targets mobile devices with deceptive emails or messages to steal credentials or initiate connections
stage.description: In this phase, the threat actor leverages phishing by crafting deceptive emails delivered via various methods to employees in the communications sector, aiming to trick them into revealing login credentials or clicking malicious links on internet-facing assets such as web servers or network interfaces.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2025-23209 (Suggested): Craft CMS suffers from improper database backup path validation, leading to code injection and remote command execution opportunities
stage.capec: CAPEC-663: Exploitation of Transient Instruction Execution — By manipulating CPU transient states, an attacker gains unauthorized access to sensitive data or bypasses security controls
stage.description: Upon successful phishing, the attacker executes arbitrary commands on the compromised endpoint through user actions, such as opening infected attachments or running downloaded scripts, exploiting mechanisms like command injection in vulnerable systems to run malicious payloads directly on the device.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-131: Resource Leak Exposure — Utilizes memory leaks or other resource issues to degrade performance and exhaust system resources, denying service to legitimate users
stage.description: With elevated access obtained from previous stages, the attacker floods communication platforms with malformed file metadata or excessive data transfers, resulting in resource forking that overwhelms servers and networks, causing denial-of-service conditions and facilitating further data collection or disruption.

Scenario.summary: This simple attack scenario begins with phishing emails sent to communication sector employees, leading to direct command execution on compromised machines, and culminating in service disruption through resource exhaustion tactics.
